title: Procris sessilis
taxonomy: Procris sessilis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae. This plant is native to tropical Africa and is often found in forests and along streams. Procris sessilis is a climber or a shrub that produces small, white flowers. The exact classification of Procris sessilis may also be considered as part of the genus Procris, which is sometimes placed in the family Urticaceae or the family Moraceae. Further research may be necessary to confirm the most accurate classification for this species.
uses: Procris sessilis, although not a widely studied plant, is believed to have various medicinal properties, with some traditional uses including treatments for fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and stems are often used in herbal remedies. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy for these purposes. Some studies suggest that Procris sessilis may possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Further investigation is necessary to fully understand its potential medicinal uses.

intro: Procris sessilis, also known as creeping Procris, is a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Procris sessilis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Procris sessilis is often used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
